$samplesNote: 3 of 264 cells. Demonstrates v0.2.0 node-tree schema (§6.3 + §6.4) on real Apollo v2 data. Full 256-cell extraction deferred. Cells chosen to exercise: SP-4 ($bindingStatus), SP-5 (swapDefaults Loading→Spinner), SP-3 (textDecoration), SP-7 (clipsContent), and the canonical fully-rounded round-trip target.
$extractionNotes: Auto-layout padding values are emitted per Figma Plugin API conventions (paddingTop/Right/Bottom/Left separately). get_design_context's `p-[…]` shorthand was lossy on this point — real Apollo v2 uses asymmetric padding (px=spacing/8, py=spacing/2 for size=default). Full extraction MUST read paddingTop/Right/Bottom/Left from Figma Plugin API directly, not infer from Tailwind code-gen output.
Constraints (horizontal/vertical) not yet captured from MCP read; defaulted to MIN/MIN. Real extraction must pull node.constraints.
Smoothing (cornerSmoothing 0…1) not captured by current MCP read; defaulted to 0. If Apollo v2 uses Apple-style continuous corners, full extraction MUST read this and emit per BUNDLE_SPEC §13.2.
Effects opacity + blendMode on individual layers defaulted; verify via use_figma plugin walk.

node.name: Variant=Default, State=Default, Size=default
node.$clipsContentReason: Default-variant container has overflow-clip set. Per SP-7 (v0.2.0), emitted explicitly even though false is the spec default.

showLeftIcon.$comment: Slot present in component tree but hidden because showLeftIcon=false (default). When true, an INSTANCE child of type 'Lucide Icons/CircleArrowLeft' appears as first child.

showKbdGroup.$comment: When true, a KbdGroup INSTANCE child appears at end. KbdGroup background variable on Default variant = {color.custom.bg-background-20-dark-bg-background-10}.

variants.$cellNotes: Loading-state cell. Tests SP-5 swapDefaults: leftIcon slot replaced by a fixed Spinner instance — `showLeftIcon` prop is hidden from the variant's interface. Also tests CORRECTION 1: fill uses primary-hover, not primary.

node.name: Variant=Default, State=Loading, Size=default
node.$geometryNote: Width 146 (vs 124 for Default state) — wider due to the always-present Spinner adding 16px + 6px gap before the label.
node.$fillBindingExplanation: Loading state intentionally uses the hover fill (primary-hover) rather than the default fill (primary). Visual cue that the button is interaction-busy. Pattern repeats across Secondary/Destructive Loading cells; Outline/Ghost/Link Loading cells keep their default fills.

children.name: Spinner
children.$slotComment: Per SP-5: Loading state replaces user-controllable leftIcon slot with a fixed Spinner instance. Hides showLeftIcon from the prop interface. SP-9 (v0.3.0): animation block carries motionReduce policy.

motionReduce.$reason: Freezing the spinner would make Loading visually identical to Default — bad UX. Slow rotation preserves activity signal at reduced motion intensity. (SP-9 v0.3.0)

showLeftIcon.$comment: Suppressed in Loading — slot occupied by mandated Spinner per SP-5 swapDefaults.

a11y.$aria-busy-required: Per Button.component.json.a11y.loadingDoublePreventionContract: Loading state MUST disable click + set aria-busy=true. Render-time consumers (e.g. Claude Design) must emit this attribute regardless of what Figma encodes.

variants.$cellNotes: Link variant. Tests SP-3 (textDecoration captured independently of typography token), SP-4 ($bindingStatus tracking when no fill is bound — transparent here), and the conceptual edge case 'pill component with no pill-shaped fill' (rounded-full still applied even though container is effectively invisible).

node.name: Variant=Link, State=Default, Size=default
node.$geometryNote: Height 32 is hardcoded (no h-* token at this value in Apollo v2). Captured per SP-4 with $bindingStatus on the layout binding.

layout.$bindingStatusReason: Padding + itemSpacing bound; height is a raw 32 with no available token at that value. Source-DS audit signal (see audit-findings-for-source.md potential follow-up: add h-8 token at 32px).

node.$fillsNote: Fill is fully transparent but still bound to a named token. Consumer renderers SHOULD honor the binding (i.e. emit `bg-transparent` or equivalent) rather than collapse to 'no background'.

cornerRadius.$cornerNote: Link variant retains rounded-full radius even though fill is transparent. Bundle preserves this faithfully; consumers MAY treat it as a no-op render.

node.$clipsContentReason: Link variant does NOT clip content. Per SP-7, emitted explicitly to make the variant-asymmetric clipping visible.

children.name: Button
children.$textDecorationNote: SP-3 (v0.2.0): textDecoration is captured INDEPENDENTLY of the typography token. boundTextStyle here is the standard (non-underlined) text-lg-leading-normal-semibold; the underline is applied on the TEXT node as a separate property. Earlier v0.1.0 spec drafts assumed an `underlined-semibold` typography token wired the look; walked-cell evidence (60:244) shows that is not how Apollo v2 implements it.

showKbdGroup.$comment: Link variant does NOT expose showKbdGroup. Variant-constraint declared in Button.component.json.variantConstraints[0].

$auditFindings.summary: Typography hard-bound to text-lg-semibold (18px/600). Breaks line rhythm when used inline in 16px regular body text.
Component documentationLink points to shadcn '#ghost' fragment instead of '#link'.

$verificationCandidate.$comment: These 3 cells double as smoke-test inputs for any downstream Skill consumer. Feed this file + tokens.json + Button.component.json to Claude Design; ask it to render each cell. Expected outcomes documented below.

expectations.expectVisual: Solid violet pill with white 'Button' label, ~124px wide. Touch-target compliant.

expectCSS: spinner child as first node, 16x16, Lucide LoaderCircle, animated 1s linear infinite
prefers-reduced-motion: rotation slowed to 4s per SP-9 motionReduce.policy=slow
expectations.expectVisual: Darker violet pill (~146px wide due to spinner), white spinner icon, white 'Button' label, aria-busy=true.

Underlined violet 'Button' text, no pill visible, no shadow. KNOWN ISSUE: when placed inline in 16px body text the 18px/semibold makes it visually heavier than surrounding type (audit-findings-for-source.md Finding 1). Bundle is faithful; source DS owns the fix.
